The specifics of your beliefs may depend on your life experiences and culture, but your commitment to them is unwavering. It sustains you and your friends as you persist through life's mysteries.
关联.application_zh: 努力理解他人的需求、意见和观点，并尝试找到共同利益和解决方案。 与不同部门和团队的人合作，促进跨部门的合作、协调和促进信息共享，保证项目的顺利进行。 积极参与行业内的网络活动和社交场合。与同行、专家和领导者建立联系，通过交流和分享经验来扩大 影响力和资源。 与他人分享自己的知识、经验和资源，帮助他人提升技能和知识水平。
关联.application_en:
Strive to understand others' needs, opinions, and perspectives, and look for shared interests and solutions.
Work with people across departments and teams to promote collaboration, coordination, and information sharing so projects run smoothly.
Actively participate in networks and social events. Connect with peers, experts, and leaders to expand your influence and resources through exchange and shared experience.
Share your knowledge, experience, and resources with others to help them grow their skills and understanding.
关联.blind_spots_zh: · 追求关联而忽视事实和逻辑的支持。将不相关的事物强行关联起来，导致关联的无效性和误导性。 · 忽视潜在的相关性，而未能发现事物之间的关联。这种遗漏可能导致对问题的理解不完整，无法获得全面的 洞察。 · 关联主题往往涉及多个因素和变量，可能过于关注某些因素，而忽视了其他重要的因素。这种偏重可能导致 对问题的解释和预测的不准确性。
关联.blind_spots_en:
· You may pursue connections while neglecting factual and logical support, forcing unrelated things together and producing invalid or misleading links.
· You may overlook potential connections and miss relationships between things, leading to incomplete understanding and less comprehensive insight.
· Connectedness often involves many factors and variables; you may focus too much on certain factors while ignoring others, reducing the accuracy of your explanations and predictions.

伯乐.standard_definition_zh: “伯乐”才干主题突出者善于发现并培养他人的潜能。 他们能够察觉任何微小的进步，并从中得到满足感。
伯乐.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Developer theme recognize and cultivate the potential in others. They spot the signs of each small improvement and derive satisfaction from evidence of progress.
伯乐.feature_zh: 欣赏他人潜力并善于发现进步的人，喜悦于细微的成长，激励他人不断进步。
伯乐.feature_en: You appreciate others' potential, notice even small improvements, and find joy in every sign of growth as you encourage people to keep progressing.
伯乐.description_zh: 尊重个体差异是你的主导特质，你对个人特质极感兴趣，不喜欢概括和典型，因为你不想忽视每个人的独 特之处。相反地，你注重个人之间的差异，自然而然地观察每个人的风格、动机、想法和人际关系建立方式。 你能听到每个人独特的故事。这个特质解释了为什么你能为朋友选择最合适的生日礼物，为什么你知道有人喜 欢公开表扬，有人却不喜欢，以及为什么你能调整教学方式以满足每个人的需求。通过细致观察他人的能力， 你让每个人都能发挥最佳水平。这种特质也有助于你组织高效的团队。当他人忙于寻找完美的团队架构或方法 时，你早已了解到伟大团队的关键在于将每个人发挥到最佳状态的适才适所。
伯乐.description_en: Respecting individual differences is your dominant trait. You are deeply interested in personal qualities and dislike generalizations or stereotypes because you do not want to overlook what makes each person unique. Instead, you focus on differences between people. You naturally observe each person's style, motivations, thoughts, and ways of building relationships. You hear each person's unique story. This trait explains why you can choose the perfect birthday gift for a friend, why you know some people welcome public praise while others do not, and why you adjust your teaching to meet each person's needs. By carefully observing others' abilities, you help everyone perform at their best. This strength also helps you build effective teams. While others search for the perfect team structure or method, you already understand that the key to a great team is placing each person where they can excel.
伯乐.application_zh: 观察和评估身边的人，发现他们的优点、才能和潜力，注意他们的成就和特长。 与他人建立良好的沟通和合作关系，了解他们的目标和需求，帮助他们发展他们的潜力，并在实现目标 的过程中提供指导和建议。 通过认可和赞赏，激发他人的动力和自信心。表达对他们的信任，并鼓励他们尝试新的挑战和机会。 通过良好的领导实践和积极的影响力，发展自己的领导能力，成为他人的榜样和引导者。 与他人建立导师关系，分享经验和知识，帮助他们发展专业能力和职业发展。
伯乐.application_en:
Observe and evaluate the people around you to discover their strengths, talents, and potential. Notice their achievements and special abilities.
Build good communication and working relationships with others. Understand their goals and needs, help them develop their potential, and offer guidance and suggestions as they work toward their objectives.
Recognize and praise others to spark their motivation and confidence. Express your trust in them and encourage them to try new challenges and opportunities.
Develop your leadership through positive influence and good practices, becoming a role model and guide for others.
Build mentoring relationships, sharing experience and knowledge to help others grow professionally.
伯乐.blind_spots_zh: · 依赖他人的赞许和认可，忽视自身的价值和成就，导致自我价值感受受损。 · 过分专注于他人的潜力和成功，忽略了自己的个人成长和发展，可能导致自身进步的缓慢。 · 对他人的成长和进步寄予过高期望，忽视每个人的独特情况和发展速度，可能带来不必要的压力和失望。
伯乐.blind_spots_en:
· You may rely too much on others' praise and recognition, neglecting your own value and achievements and damaging your self-worth.
· You may focus so much on others' potential and success that you ignore your own growth and development, slowing your own progress.
· You may hold overly high expectations for others' growth, overlooking each person's unique situation and pace, which can create unnecessary pressure and disappointment.

体谅.standard_definition_zh: “体谅”才干主题突出的人能够设身处地地体会他人的 感受。
体谅.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Empathy theme can sense the feelings of other people by imagining themselves in others' lives.
体谅.feature_zh: 具备同理心的人是心灵导师，善于感同身受，体察他人情感，以温暖他人心灵。
体谅.feature_en: You are a natural emotional guide, able to put yourself in others' shoes, sense their feelings, and warm their hearts with understanding.
体谅.description_zh: 你善于洞察他人。你能够像体会自己一样感受到他们的情感。你直觉地透过他们的眼睛看世界，并与他们 分享观点。你并非赞同每个人的看法，也并非同情每个人的困境 ——这是体谅而非怜悯。你可能不同意每个人 的选择，但你确实理解他们，而这种天生的理解力是非常强大的。你能听到无声的疑问，预测他人的需求。而 别人为了找到合适的词句而苦思冥想时，你总能准确地找到恰如其分的措辞和语气。你帮助他人寻找适当的言 辞，以表达自己或他人的情感。你帮助他们倾诉自己的情感生活。正因为这些原因，其他人对你产生了吸引 力。
体谅.description_en: You are skilled at reading others. You can feel their emotions as if they were your own. You intuitively see the world through their eyes and share their perspective. This does not mean you agree with every view or pity every plight—this is empathy, not sympathy. You may not agree with every choice someone makes, but you do understand them, and this natural understanding is powerful. You hear unspoken questions and anticipate others' needs. While others struggle to find the right words, you can pinpoint exactly the right phrasing and tone. You help others find appropriate words to express their own or others' feelings. You help them give voice to their emotional lives. For these reasons, people are drawn to you.
体谅.application_zh: 积极倾听他人的意见和观点，尊重他人的感受和想法，表达出对他人感受的关心和共鸣。 尊重和接纳不同个体的差异，包括文化背景、观念和工作风格等，鼓励多样性和不同声音的表达。 关注他人的困难和挑战，愿意伸出援手并提供实质性的帮助，共同解决问题并实现共同目标。 关注并回应他人的需求。表达对他人的关心和支持，提供合适的帮助和资源，以满足他人的需求，并促 进他人的成长和发展。
体谅.application_en:
Listen actively to others' opinions and perspectives, respect their feelings and ideas, and show genuine care and resonance for what they feel.
Respect and accept differences among individuals, including cultural backgrounds, values, and working styles, and encourage diversity and diverse voices.
Pay attention to others' difficulties and challenges, be willing to lend a hand, and provide practical help to solve problems together and achieve shared goals.
Notice and respond to others' needs. Express care and support, offer appropriate help and resources, and promote others' growth and development.
体谅.blind_spots_zh: · 过度假设他人的感受可能导致误解和误判，无法真正理解他人的需求和情感。 · 自我牺牲和忽视自己的需求和边界，使自己处于不利的境地。 · 不敢表达自己的意见和需求，忽视了良好的沟通和建立互相理解的机会。
体谅.blind_spots_en:
· Assuming too much about how others feel can lead to misunderstanding and misjudgment, preventing you from truly grasping their needs and emotions.
· You may sacrifice yourself and neglect your own needs and boundaries, putting yourself at a disadvantage.
· You may hesitate to express your own opinions and needs, missing opportunities for good communication and mutual understanding.

和谐.standard_definition_zh: “和谐”才干主题突出的人渴求协调一致。 他们避免冲突，寻求共识。
和谐.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Harmony theme look for consensus. They don't enjoy conflict; rather, they seek areas of agreement.
和谐.feature_zh: 和谐主题突出的人追求协调与共识，像和平天使一样化解冲突、营造和谐。
和谐.feature_en: You pursue coordination and consensus, resolving conflict and creating harmony like a peacemaker.
和谐.description_zh: 你追求共识。你认为冲突和摩擦是有害的，所以你努力将其降至最低。当你发现周围的人意见不一致时， 你力求达成共同点，同时尊重彼此的差异。和谐是你的核心价值观。尽管有人总是试图强加自己的观点，你对 此感到难以置信。你相信如果我们避免固执己见，而是寻求共识和支持，将会带来更大的效益。你坚信这一 点，并且以此原则对待他人和处理事务。当别人强调他们的目标、权益和信仰时，你选择保持沉默。当别人朝 着某个方向前进时，为了维持和谐，你愿意调整自己的目标以与他们保持一致（前提是基本价值相符）。当别 人对他们钟爱的理论或方法大谈特谈时，你避免争论，更愿意讨论可能建立共识的实际问题。在你看来，我们 都在同一艘船上，必须团结合作。这艘船是好船，没有必要为了显示实力而使它晃动不安。
和谐.description_en: You seek consensus. You see conflict and friction as harmful, so you work to minimize them. When you find people around you disagreeing, you strive to find common ground while respecting differences. Harmony is one of your core values. You find it hard to believe when some people insist on imposing their views. You believe that if we avoid stubbornly holding to our own positions and instead seek consensus and support, we will achieve greater results. You hold this belief firmly and apply it in how you treat others and handle matters. When others emphasize their goals, interests, and beliefs, you choose to remain silent. When others move in a certain direction, you are willing to adjust your own goals to align with them, as long as fundamental values are not compromised. When others passionately defend their favorite theories or methods, you avoid argument and prefer to discuss practical matters on which consensus can be built. In your view, we are all in the same boat and must work together. It is a good boat; there is no need to rock it just to show strength.
和谐.application_zh: 确保与周围人之间有开放、透明和及时的沟通渠道，避免信息不对称和误解，增强合作和和谐。 重视他人的观点和感受，积极倾听他人的想法和建议，展示出真诚的关怀和关心。 鼓励团队成员之间的合作和协作，强调团队的共同目标和利益，共同努力解决问题和实现目标。 处理冲突时采取积极的方法，鼓励开放的对话和理解，寻求妥协和共赢的解决方案，以维护和谐的关 系。
和谐.application_en:
Make sure open, transparent, and timely communication channels exist with the people around you. Avoid information gaps and misunderstandings, and strengthen cooperation and harmony.
Value others' perspectives and feelings. Listen actively to their ideas and suggestions and show sincere care and concern.
Encourage teamwork and collaboration among team members, emphasizing shared goals and interests so everyone works together to solve problems and achieve objectives.
Approach conflict constructively by encouraging open dialogue and understanding, seeking compromise and win-win solutions, and preserving harmonious relationships.
和谐.blind_spots_zh: · 合理的冲突可以带来创新和改进，过度追求和谐可能忽视了冲突的积极作用。 · 可能导致忽视个人的需求和边界，无法有效地满足自己的利益和利益。 · 压抑自己的情绪和需求，可能使得问题无法得到解决和满足。
和谐.blind_spots_en:
· Healthy conflict can drive innovation and improvement; pursuing harmony too strongly may overlook the positive role of conflict.
· It may lead you to neglect your own needs and boundaries, making it hard to protect your own interests.
· Suppressing your own emotions and needs may prevent problems from being resolved and needs from being met.

包容.standard_definition_zh: 包容力强的人善于接纳他人。他们关心那些被忽略的人， 并努力让这些人融入集体。
包容.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Includer theme accept others. They show awareness of those who feel left out and make efforts to include them.
包容.feature_zh: 包容力强的人是慈善家，他们善于接纳他人，关心被忽视的人，并促使他们融入集体。
包容.feature_en: You are naturally accepting, caring about those who are overlooked and helping them become part of the group.
包容.description_zh: "广纳众人 "是你的人生信条。你希望将人们吸引到团队中，让他们感到自己是团队的一员。有些人只加入 排他性的小团体，而你则不同，你避免任何排斥的团体。你渴望扩大团队，让尽可能多的人从团队的支持中受 益。你不愿看到任何人被孤立在团体之外，因为孤立的感觉是多么寒冷。你会说： “扩大圈子，让他们加入， 并给予他们温暖。 ”你天生具备包容性，无论是种族、性别、国籍、性格还是信仰，你从不随意评判。评判可 能伤害他人的感情，除非迫不得已，为什么要这样做呢？你之所以对每个人都敞开心扉，并不仅仅是因为你相 信人与人之间有差异，因此应该尊重彼此的差异，而是因为你确信在本质上我们都是相同的。我们都是独特而 重要的。因此，没有人应该被忽视。每个人都有自己的份量，这是我们的基本权利。
包容.description_en: "Include everyone" is your life's motto. You want to draw people into the team and help them feel like members. While some people join exclusive cliques, you avoid any group that excludes others. You want to expand the team so that as many people as possible benefit from its support. You hate to see anyone isolated from the group, because loneliness is a cold feeling. You would say, "Widen the circle, invite them in, and give them warmth." You are naturally inclusive. Whether race, gender, nationality, personality, or belief, you do not judge casually. Judgment can hurt others' feelings, so why do it unless absolutely necessary? You open your heart to everyone not only because you believe people are different and should respect those differences, but because you are convinced that at our core we are the same. We are all unique and important. Therefore, no one should be overlooked. Everyone matters, and that is our fundamental right.
包容.application_zh: 展现对不同背景、观点和经验的尊重和欣赏。认识到每个人都有独特的贡献和价值，倾听并接纳不同意 见和观点。 主动提供支持和帮助他人的机会，展现关心和同理心。愿意倾听他人的需求，并努力为他人提供支持和 协助。 鼓励积极的沟通和反馈，提供安全和开放的环境，让每个人都能表达意见和感受。确保沟通渠道畅通， 包括定期会议、团队讨论和个人反馈。 加强自我意识，了解自己的偏好和偏见，积极反思和调整自己的行为，加强与他人的情感连接和共情能 力。
包容.application_en:
Show respect and appreciation for different backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. Recognize that everyone has unique contributions and value, and listen to and accept differing opinions and views.
Actively offer opportunities to support and help others, showing care and empathy. Be willing to listen to others' needs and do your best to provide support and assistance.
Encourage positive communication and feedback by providing a safe and open environment where everyone can express opinions and feelings. Make sure communication channels stay open, including regular meetings, team discussions, and personal feedback.
Strengthen self-awareness by understanding your own preferences and biases, reflecting actively, and adjusting your behavior to build stronger emotional connections and empathy with others.
包容.blind_spots_zh: · 对于不同文化背景、价值观和行为规范的认知不足，缺乏对多元文化的尊重和理解。 · 与同质化的社交圈子保持较高的亲密度，缺乏与多样化背景的人交流和接触的机会。 · 身处不支持包容和多样性的环境，如歧视性言论和行为较多的地方，会对包容主题产生负面影响。
包容.blind_spots_en:
· You may lack knowledge of different cultural backgrounds, values, and behavioral norms, leading to insufficient respect and understanding of diversity.
· You may stay closely connected to a homogeneous social circle and miss opportunities to interact with people from diverse backgrounds.
· Being in an environment that does not support inclusion and diversity, such as one with frequent discriminatory words or actions, can weaken your Includer theme.

个别.standard_definition_zh: “个别”才干主题突出的人对每个人的独有特质兴趣盎然。 他们善于发现如何令个性迥异的人一起高效地共事。
个别.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Individualization theme are intrigued with the unique qualities of each person. They have a gift for figuring out how different people can work together productively.
个别.feature_zh: 个别主题较强的人对每个人的与众不同之处兴趣盎然。他们善于琢磨如何将个性迥异的人组合在一起，创 造出最大成效。
个别.feature_en: You are fascinated by what makes each person different. You naturally figure out how to combine people with distinct personalities so they can produce the greatest results together.
个别.description_zh: 你对每个人的独特之处充满兴趣。你无法容忍对所有人进行笼统的概括或简单的分类。你深知这样做会模 糊个人特质。相反，你注重个人差异。你本能地观察每个人的风格、动机、思维方式和交际方式，倾听他们独 一无二的生活经历。这种能力完全展示了你与他人相处的方式。它解释了为什么你能选择恰到好处的生日礼 物；为什么你知道有些人喜欢而有些人讨厌公开表扬；为什么你能根据不同的人施以教诲，有时手把手地指 导，有时则鼓励他们自己思考。通过密切观察他人的优势，你能够让每个人充分发挥潜力。你深知通过告诉一 个人你所看到的具体优势，他 /她将继续发掘自己的潜力。这种对个体的关注还有助于你建立高效的团队。有 些人追求完美无缺的团队结构或流程，而你的直觉告诉你，优秀团队的秘诀在于让每个成员发挥自己最擅长的 工作。
个别.description_en: You are genuinely interested in what makes each person unique. You cannot tolerate sweeping generalizations or simple categories because you know they blur individual traits. Instead, you focus on personal differences. You instinctively observe each person's style, motivations, ways of thinking, and ways of relating. You listen to their one-of-a-kind life stories. This ability shapes how you relate to others. It explains why you choose just the right birthday gift, why you know some people love public praise while others hate it, and why you teach differently depending on the person—sometimes guiding them step by step, other times encouraging them to think for themselves. By closely observing others' strengths, you help each person reach their full potential. You know that when you tell someone the specific strengths you see in them, they will continue to develop those gifts. This focus on individuals also helps you build high-performing teams. While some people search for a perfect team structure or process, your intuition tells you that the secret to an excellent team is letting each member do what they do best.
个别.application_zh: 了解自己的个别优势，并认识到它们在工作和生活中的价值和影响力。参考优势评估或咨询专家的意 见，帮助您更好地理解自己的优势。 将个别优势与工作和生活中的目标相结合。设定具体的目标和挑战，利用个别优势来克服难题和实现成 就感。 主动寻找能够应用个人优势的项目、任务或机会。与领导者或团队成员沟通，表达您对某个特定任务或 项目的兴趣，并解释如何能够发挥自己的个别优势。
个别.application_en:
Understand your own individual strengths and recognize their value and impact in your work and life. Use strength assessments or consult experts to better understand what makes you unique.
Connect your individual strengths to goals in your work and life. Set specific goals and challenges, and use your strengths to overcome difficulties and achieve a sense of accomplishment.
Proactively look for projects, tasks, or opportunities where you can apply your personal strengths. Communicate with leaders or team members to express interest in a specific task or project and explain how your unique strengths can contribute.
个别.blind_spots_zh: · 对于个别差异抱有偏见和歧视态度，缺乏对个体独特性的尊重和包容。 · 无视个体的沟通需求和方式，忽略有效的沟通和理解对个体的重要性。 · 缺乏与他人建立共情和理解的能力，无法体验和理解他人的感受和经历。
个别.blind_spots_en:
· You may hold prejudiced or discriminatory attitudes toward individual differences, lacking respect and acceptance for what makes each person unique.
· You may ignore individuals' communication needs and styles, overlooking the importance of effective communication and understanding for each person.
· You may lack the ability to build empathy and understanding with others, making it hard to experience and appreciate their feelings and experiences.

积极.standard_definition_zh: 积极的人浑身充满了富有感染力的热情。 他们乐观向上，并能感召周围的人。
积极.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Positivity theme have an enthusiasm that is contagious. They are upbeat and can get others excited about what they are going to do.
积极.feature_zh: 积极的人散发着富有感染力的热情，以快乐和积极向上的态度激励周围的人。
积极.feature_en: You radiate contagious enthusiasm, inspiring those around you with joy and a positive, forward-looking attitude.
积极.description_zh: 你慷慨赞美他人，笑容满面，善于抓住幽默时机。有人说你毫无忧虑。还有人希望能像你一样乐观豁达。 总之，人们喜欢和你在一起。有了你，他们的世界变得更美好，因为你的热情充满感染力。缺少了你的活力和 乐观，有些人感到他们的世界变得重复和乏味，甚至充满压力。你似乎总能给他们的心灵注入活力。你为每个 项目注入欢乐，庆祝每一项成就。你尽力让每件事都充满生机和激情。由于你充满精力，某些玩世不恭的人可 能排斥你，但你的积极态度很少让你失望。你内心深信生活美好无比，工作充满乐趣。无论遇到什么挫折，你 都不会失去幽默感。
积极.description_en: You praise others generously, smile easily, and have a gift for timing humor well. Some say you have no worries. Others wish they could be as optimistic and open-minded as you. In short, people enjoy being around you. With you, their world becomes brighter because your energy is infectious. Without your vitality and optimism, some people feel their world becomes repetitive, dull, and even stressful. You always seem to breathe new life into their hearts. You bring joy to every project and celebrate every achievement. You try to make everything feel alive and exciting. Because you are so full of energy, some cynical people may push you away, but your positive attitude rarely lets you down. Deep down you believe life is wonderful and work is fun. No matter what setbacks you face, you never lose your sense of humor.
积极.application_zh: 关注自己内心的对话，并将其转化为积极的、励志的声音。培养自信和自尊，相信自己的能力和潜力。 以积极的心态对待工作中的挑战和困难。寻找解决问题的方法和机会，抱有成长的心态，相信自己能够 克服困难并取得成功。 与积极向上的人为伍，建立支持和积极的人际关系。分享积极的经历和观点，与他人共享快乐和成功， 鼓励和激励他人。 注重自己的身心健康，保持良好的生活习惯。合理安排工作和休息时间，参与锻炼、放松和充电的活 动，保持积极的生活态度。
积极.application_en:
Pay attention to your inner dialogue and turn it into a positive, encouraging voice. Build confidence and self-respect by believing in your abilities and potential.
Approach challenges and difficulties with a positive mindset. Look for ways to solve problems and seize opportunities. Adopt a growth mindset and believe you can overcome obstacles and succeed.
Surround yourself with positive people and build supportive, uplifting relationships. Share positive experiences and perspectives, spread joy and success with others, and encourage and inspire them.
Take care of your physical and mental health and maintain good habits. Balance work and rest, exercise, relax, and recharge so you can keep a positive attitude toward life.
积极.blind_spots_zh: · 过于积极的人可能忽视现实中的问题和困难，对挑战和障碍持过于乐观的态度，导致缺乏实际的解决方案。 · 有时候，过于强调积极的态度可能导致表面上的积极，而忽视真实的情感和需求，造成内心的不满和不适。 · 积极主题的人可能过于追求成功和进步，过度竞争和自我比较，导致焦虑和不满足感。
积极.blind_spots_en:
· Being overly positive may cause you to overlook real problems and difficulties, approach challenges with excessive optimism, and fail to develop practical solutions.
· Sometimes emphasizing a positive attitude too strongly can create a surface-level positivity that ignores real emotions and needs, causing inner dissatisfaction and discomfort.
· People with strong Positivity may pursue success and progress so intensely that they become overly competitive and compare themselves too much with others, leading to anxiety and a sense of inadequacy.

交往.standard_definition_zh: 交往能力强的人喜欢人际间的亲密关系。 他们最大的满足是与朋友一道为实现一个目标而共同努力。
交往.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Relator theme enjoy close relationships with others. They find deep satisfaction in working hard with friends to achieve a goal.
交往.feature_zh: 交往能力强的人享受亲密的人际关系。他们最大的乐趣是与朋友一起合作，共同实现共同目标。
交往.feature_en: You enjoy close, intimate relationships. Your greatest pleasure comes from working alongside friends toward a shared goal.
交往.description_zh: 交往主题的你与熟人之间的关系更加紧密。虽然你未必会拒绝结交新朋友，事实上，你可能有其他主题使 你在结交新朋友时感到乐趣无穷。然而，你从与亲密友人的交往中获得巨大的欢乐和力量。你对亲密关系感到 自然而顺其自然。实际上，一旦你结识了某人，你就会有意深化这段关系。你敞开心扉与朋友交心，同时也希 望成为他们的知己。你渴望了解他们的情感、目标、恐惧和梦想；同时，你也希望他们以同样的方式了解你。 你深知这种亲密关系并非没有风险，他人可能会利用你，但你愿意接受这种风险。对你而言，只有真诚的关系 才有价值。而真诚关系的唯一检验方式就是完全信任对方。你愿意与他人分享更多，因为这样你们承担的风险 也更多。而风险越多，你们之间的关爱就越能得到验证。这就是你建立真诚友谊的步骤，而你对此是心甘情愿 的。
交往.description_en: With the Relator theme, your relationships with familiar people run deeper. Although you may not refuse to make new friends—in fact, you may have other themes that make meeting new people fun—you gain enormous joy and strength from being with close friends. Close relationships feel natural and effortless to you. In fact, once you meet someone, you intentionally deepen the connection. You open your heart to friends and want to be their confidant. You long to understand their emotions, goals, fears, and dreams, and you want them to know yours in the same way. You know such intimacy carries risk; others may take advantage of you, but you are willing to accept that risk. To you, only genuine relationships have value. The only test of a genuine relationship is complete trust. You are willing to share more of yourself because the more risk you both take, the more your care for each other is proven. This is how you build authentic friendships, and you do it willingly.
交往.application_zh: 愿意与他人分享知识和经验，支持他人的成长和发展。 尊重和接纳他人的不同观点、文化和背景。倡导多样性和包容性，并与不同背景的人合作，增进相互理 解和合作。 重视他人的观点和感受。倾听他人的需求和问题，并展现出真正的关心。 培养良好的社交技巧，学会察言观色，适应不同的人际交往风格，以更好地理解和与他人沟通。 培养解决问题的技巧，处理冲突时保持冷静和客观，寻求妥协和解决方案，有效沟通，寻求共同的利益 和理解。
交往.application_en:
Be willing to share your knowledge and experience with others to support their growth and development.
Respect and accept others' different viewpoints, cultures, and backgrounds. Advocate for diversity and inclusion, and work with people from diverse backgrounds to increase mutual understanding and cooperation.
Value others' perspectives and feelings. Listen to their needs and problems and show genuine care.
Develop good social skills. Learn to read the room and adapt to different interpersonal styles so you can better understand and communicate with others.
Build problem-solving skills. Stay calm and objective when handling conflict, seek compromise and solutions, communicate effectively, and look for shared interests and understanding.
交往.blind_spots_zh: · 过于坚持己见和固定的交往模式，不愿意适应和调整自己的行为和观念，影响交往的互动性和流畅度。 · 忽视寻找共同的利益点和共同目标，导致交往关系的表面化和短暂性。 · 不尊重他人的个人空间和隐私，过于侵入性的行为和过问，造成他人的不适和疏离。
交往.blind_spots_en:
· You may cling too tightly to your own views and fixed ways of relating, resisting adapting or adjusting your behavior and beliefs, which can make interactions less fluid.
· You may neglect to find shared interests and common goals, causing relationships to become superficial and short-lived.
· You may fail to respect others' personal space and privacy, behaving intrusively or asking overly personal questions, which can make others uncomfortable and distant.

思维.standard_definition_zh: 思维能力较强的人最大的特点是他们善于思考。 他们勤于自省，敏于探讨。
思维.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Intellection theme are characterized by their intellectual activity. They are introspective and appreciate intellectual discussions.
思维.feature_zh: 思维能力突出的人善于思考，勤于自我反省和积极探讨问题。
思维.feature_en: People with strong Intellection talents enjoy thinking deeply. They are naturally reflective and love engaging in thoughtful discussions.
思维.description_zh: 你热衷于思考，喜欢思维的活跃。你喜欢锻炼你的大脑，挑战它的边界。对思维活动的需求可能是有针对 性的，具体的焦点取决于你的其他优势。你可能致力于解决问题、孕育创意或理解他人的感受。另一方面，你 的思维活动也可能是广泛的。思考的主题并不局限于特定的内容，它只表示你喜欢思考。你喜欢独处，因为这 样可以进行深思熟虑。你性格内向。在某种程度上，你是自己最好的伴侣，因为你会反思并测试自己的答案。 当你将自己的实际行动与思考中的各种想法相比较时，你可能会感到些许不满。但这种自省也可能使你关注现 实问题，比如当天发生的事件或即将进行的谈话。不论它将你引向何方，持续的思考是你生活中不可或缺的一 部分。
思维.description_en: You are fascinated by thinking. You enjoy exercising your mind, stretching its boundaries. Your need for mental activity may be focused, with the specific subject matter depending on your other talents. You may dedicate yourself to solving problems, generating ideas, or understanding other people's feelings. On the other hand, your thinking may be broad and unfocused. The Intellection theme is not limited to a particular content; it simply means you enjoy thinking. You like spending time alone because it allows you to reflect deeply. In a way, you are your own best companion, testing your answers against yourself. You may feel slightly dissatisfied when you compare your actual actions with the many ideas you have explored in your mind. But this introspection can also keep you grounded in reality, focused on events of the day or an upcoming conversation. Wherever it leads you, continuous thinking is an essential part of your life.
思维.application_zh: 采用多种思维方式和角度来看待问题。如逆向思维、横向思维和系统思维，以获得全面的洞察和创新的 解决方案。 培养创造性思维能力，发掘新的想法和解决方案。鼓励思维的自由流动，尝试新的方法和观点，勇于提 出新的想法并将其付诸实践。 培养批判性思维能力，对问题进行深入分析和评估。质疑和审查现有的假设和观点，挑战常规思维，并 提出合理的质疑和反驳。 运用系统思维的能力，将问题放置在更广阔的背景中考虑，并理解各个因素之间的相互关系和影响，并 思考长期和综合的影响。 运用思维优势主题来解决问题，包括定义问题、收集信息、分析和评估选项，，找到切实可行的解决方 案并实施。
思维.application_en:
Use a variety of thinking approaches and perspectives to look at problems — such as reverse thinking, lateral thinking, and systems thinking — to gain comprehensive insight and innovative solutions.
Cultivate creative thinking skills to discover new ideas and solutions. Encourage the free flow of thought, try new methods and viewpoints, and be bold in proposing and putting new ideas into practice.
Develop critical thinking skills to analyse and evaluate problems in depth. Question existing assumptions and viewpoints, challenge conventional thinking, and put forward reasonable doubts and counter-arguments.
Use systems thinking to place problems in a broader context, understand the interrelationships and influences among factors, and consider long-term and comprehensive effects.
Apply your Intellection talents to problem-solving: define the problem, gather information, analyse and evaluate options, find practical solutions, and implement them.
思维.blind_spots_zh: · 陷入一成不变的思维模式，难以开拓新的思路和解决问题的方法。 · 无法将问题放在更广阔的系统和整体的背景中考虑，忽视了问题的相互关联和综合性。 · 过于自信和确定自己的观点是正确的，而不愿意接受其他可能的解释或观点。
思维.blind_spots_en:
You may fall into fixed patterns of thinking and find it hard to open up new ideas or problem-solving methods.
You may fail to place problems in a broader systems context, neglecting their interconnections and complexity.
You may be overconfident and certain that your viewpoint is correct, unwilling to accept other possible explanations or perspectives.

理念.standard_definition_zh: “理念”才干主题突出的人痴迷于各种奇思妙想。 他们能够从看似毫无关联的现象中找到彼此之间的联系。
理念.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Ideation theme are fascinated by ideas. They are able to find connections between seemingly disparate phenomena.
理念.feature_zh: 他们对各种理念着迷，能够从看似毫无关联的现象中发现它们之间的相互关系。
理念.feature_en: They are fascinated by ideas and can discover connections between seemingly unrelated phenomena.
理念.description_zh: 你对理念执着迷恋。理念即概念，是对大部分事件最合理解释的表达。当你透过复杂的表面发现一个简明 精彩的概念，进而揭示事物本质时，你会欣喜若狂。理念是关联的体现，你的思维总是在寻找关联；因此，当 不同现象被一个微不足道的纽带联系起来时，你会感到新奇。理念是对习以为常的挑战，带来全新的见解。你 乐于将我们熟悉的世界转个弯，从一个陌生但新颖的角度审视它。你喜欢所有这些理念，因为它们深邃，新 颖，能追溯到根本，引发争议，充满奇思妙想。出于这些原因，每当你产生一个新理念时，你都会兴奋不已。 别人可能视你为富有创新精神、与众不同、理性或聪明绝伦。或许这些都是对的。谁能说得准呢？但你确信不 疑的是，理念让你激动不已。对于大多数日子来说，这已经足够了。
理念.description_en: You are fascinated by ideas. An idea is a concept, the best explanation of most events. When you look through a complex surface and discover a simple, brilliant concept that reveals the essence of things, you are thrilled. Ideas are about connections; your mind is constantly searching for them. So when different phenomena are linked by the smallest thread, you find it novel. Ideas challenge the familiar and bring fresh insights. You enjoy turning our familiar world upside down and examining it from a strange but new angle. You love all these ideas because they are profound, novel, rooted in fundamentals, controversial, and wonderfully creative. For these reasons, whenever a new idea occurs to you, you become excited. Others may see you as innovative, different, rational, or exceptionally clever. Perhaps all of these are true. Who can say? But what you know for certain is that ideas excite you. For most days, that is enough.
理念.application_zh: 花时间思考和定义自己的核心价值观和人生目标。这将帮助您在工作和生活中保持一致性，并使决策更 加有针对性和有意义。 寻找与自己价值观和理念相符的工作，或者在现有工作中找到与之相关的方面。将自己的使命融入到工 作中，让工作成为实现个人理念的途径。 与他人分享您的观点和见解，通过演讲、写作、教育等方式传播您的理念，争取更大的影响力。 如果您是团队的一员或领导者，努力营造一个共同理念和目标的团队文化，激发团队的凝聚力和合作精 神。
理念.application_en:
Spend time thinking about and defining your core values and life goals. This will help you maintain consistency in work and life and make your decisions more purposeful and meaningful.
Seek work that aligns with your values and ideas, or find aspects of your current work that connect with them. Integrate your mission into your work so that it becomes a pathway to realising your personal ideals.
Share your views and insights with others through speaking, writing, teaching, and other means to expand your influence.
If you are a team member or leader, work to build a team culture with shared ideals and goals, inspiring cohesion and a spirit of cooperation.
理念.blind_spots_zh: · 陷入过于理想化的情境和理论框架中，而忽视现实的限制和挑战。可能追求完美的理念，但忽略了实际可行 性和可实施性。 · 如果倾向于简化和抽象化理念，而忽视了其中的多元性和复杂性，这种简化可能导致理念的不完整性和误导 性。 · 忽视不同文化和价值观之间的差异，将自己的理念强加于他人，导致理念的冲突和难以接受。
理念.blind_spots_en:
You may become trapped in overly idealised scenarios and theoretical frameworks, ignoring real-world limitations and challenges. You may pursue a perfect idea while overlooking practical feasibility and implementability.
If you tend to simplify and abstract ideas, you may neglect their diversity and complexity. This simplification can lead to incomplete and misleading ideas.
You may ignore differences in culture and values, imposing your ideas on others and causing conflict and resistance.

学习.standard_definition_zh: 学习能力强的人有旺盛的求知欲，渴望不断提高自我。 令他们激动的是求知的过程而非结果。
学习.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Learner theme have a great desire to learn and want to continuously improve. The process of learning, rather than the outcome, excites them.
学习.feature_zh: 学习能力强的人具备强烈求知欲望，对知识的获取过程更加激动人心，而非仅关注结果。
学习.feature_en: People with strong Learner talents have a strong desire to learn. They are more excited by the process of gaining knowledge than by the result alone.
学习.description_zh: 你热爱学习。你对学习的热情不仅受到你其他兴趣和经历的影响，而且无论是哪个学科，你总是被学习的 过程所吸引。对你而言，过程比内容或结果更加令人兴奋。从无知到熟练的扎实而执着的学习过程能让你倍感 精力充沛。你喜欢初学的喜悦，通过背诵或实践所学内容来提升自己，以及掌握新技能时日益增长的自信心。 这些学习过程深深吸引着你。你对学习的激情促使你参加各种成人教育课程，例如太极拳、外语、钢琴、舞蹈 或计算机培训等。它使你能够在不断变化的环境中游刃有余，无论是应对紧急项目的安排，还是迅速成为某一 新领域的专家要求。对你而言，学习的主题并不一定意味着追求成为某个领域的专家或追求与特定专业或学术 头衔相关的荣誉。相反，对你来说，更重要的是学习的旅程，而不仅仅是具体的结果。
学习.description_en: You love to learn. Your passion for learning is shaped by your other interests and experiences, but whatever the subject, you are drawn to the process of learning. For you, the process is more exciting than the content or the result. The solid, persistent journey from ignorance to competence energises you. You enjoy the thrill of being a beginner, improving yourself through memorisation or practice, and the growing confidence that comes with mastering a new skill. These learning processes deeply attract you. Your passion for learning drives you to take all kinds of adult education courses — tai chi, foreign languages, piano, dance, or computer training, for example. It enables you to navigate changing environments with ease, whether coordinating an urgent project or quickly becoming the go-to expert in a new field. For you, the Learner theme does not necessarily mean striving to become an expert in a particular field or pursuing honours tied to a specific profession or academic title. Rather, what matters most to you is the journey of learning, not just the concrete outcome.
学习.application_zh: 将学到的知识和技能应用于工作和生活中，将学习转化为实际行动。找到机会将学习成果展示和分享， 为团队和组织带来积极影响。 保持好奇心和开放心态，积极主动地探索新的领域和知识。参与跨部门项目、交叉学科的讨论和合作， 拓宽自己的视野和经验。 掌握有效的学习方法和技巧，如提取关键信息、整理笔记、积极参与讨论、反思和总结等。寻找适合自 己的学习方式，提高学习效率和成果。
学习.application_en:
Apply the knowledge and skills you learn to your work and life. Turn learning into action. Find opportunities to demonstrate and share your learning outcomes, creating a positive impact for your team and organisation.
Maintain curiosity and an open mind. Actively explore new fields and knowledge. Participate in cross-departmental projects and interdisciplinary discussions and collaborations to broaden your horizons and experience.
Master effective learning methods and techniques, such as extracting key information, organising notes, actively participating in discussions, reflecting, and summarising. Find the learning style that suits you best to improve efficiency and results.
学习.blind_spots_zh: · 忽视学习和掌握基础知识的重要性，直接进入高级概念和技能，导致学习的薄弱环节和不牢固的基础。 · 选择不适合自己的学习方法，导致学习效率低下或学习成果不理想。 · 只停留在理论层面，不进行实践和应用，无法将所学知识转化为实际能力和技能。
学习.blind_spots_en:
You may neglect the importance of learning and mastering foundational knowledge, jumping straight into advanced concepts and skills. This can lead to weak links and an insecure foundation in your learning.
You may choose learning methods that do not suit you, resulting in low efficiency or unsatisfactory outcomes.
You may stay at the theoretical level without practising or applying what you have learned, preventing your knowledge from being turned into real ability and skill.

分析.standard_definition_zh: 分析能力强的人喜欢探究事物的来龙去脉。 他们有能力思考可能影响局面的诸多因素。
分析.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Analytical theme search for reasons and causes. They have the ability to think about all of the factors that might affect a situation.
分析.feature_zh: 具有强大分析能力的人喜欢深入探究事物的来龙去脉，能够综合考虑各种因素对局势的影响。
分析.feature_en: People with strong Analytical talents enjoy exploring the reasons behind things. They are able to consider many factors that affect a situation.
分析.description_zh: 你向他人提出挑战： “证明它。告诉我为什么你的结论是正确的。 ”面对这种质疑，一些人会发现他们自以 为是的理论逐渐失去力量和支撑。而这正是你的意图。你并非要扼杀别人的观点，但你坚信他们的理论必须经 得起检验。你以客观和冷静自居。你喜欢数据，因为它们不包含特定的价值观，也没有特殊的目的，并且没有 被修饰过。通过数据的支持，你寻找模式和关联。你想了解不同模式之间的相互影响。你的问题是：它们如何 组合？会有什么结果？这个结果是否与提出的理论或所面临的情况相符？你逐层剖析，直至揭示其中的根源。 他人认为你的思维逻辑严密而严谨。久而久之，他们习惯于请你运用严谨的思维方式来剖析那些 “异想天 开”和“愚钝 ”的观点。但愿你的分析不要过于尖刻。否则，如果他们自己的想法属于 “异想天开 ”，他们可能会 故意回避你。
分析.description_en: You challenge others: "Prove it. Tell me why your conclusion is correct." Faced with this challenge, some people find their supposedly solid theories gradually losing strength and support. That is your intention. You are not trying to kill other people's ideas, but you firmly believe their theories must stand up to scrutiny. You see yourself as objective and cool-headed. You like data because they contain no specific values, no hidden agenda, and no embellishment. Supported by data, you look for patterns and connections. You want to understand how different patterns influence one another. Your questions are: How do they fit together? What will happen? Does the result match the proposed theory or the situation at hand? You peel back layer after layer until you reach the root. Others see your thinking as logical and rigorous. Over time, they become used to asking you to apply your rigorous thinking to cut through "wild" and "foolish" ideas. Just hope your analysis does not become too sharp. Otherwise, if their own ideas fall into the "wild" category, they may deliberately avoid you.
分析.application_zh: 积极收集相关的信息和数据，利用各种资源，包括书籍、报告、研究和互联网，收集全面的信息。 运用逻辑思维和分析能力，对收集到的数据进行分析和解读，从中获取有价值的见解。 基于分析的结果，提出假设和推理，推导出合理的结论和预测。 在做出决策之前，评估潜在的风险和机会，制定相应的应对策略。 根据分析的结果，制定决策和行动计划。将决策细化为具体的步骤和时间表，确保执行的可行性和有效 性。 实施决策后，密切监测执行的结果 ,定期评估决策的效果，并根据反馈进行调整和改进。
分析.application_en:
Actively collect relevant information and data. Use a variety of resources, including books, reports, research, and the internet, to gather comprehensive information.
Use logical thinking and analytical skills to analyse and interpret the data you collect, deriving valuable insights from it.
Based on your analysis, put forward hypotheses and inferences, deriving reasonable conclusions and predictions.
Before making decisions, assess potential risks and opportunities and develop corresponding response strategies.
Based on your analysis, make decisions and action plans. Break decisions down into concrete steps and timelines to ensure feasible and effective execution.
After implementation, closely monitor the results and regularly evaluate the effectiveness of your decisions, making adjustments and improvements based on feedback.
分析.blind_spots_zh: · 选择性地采集和使用数据，忽视或排除了其他重要的信息和数据源。 · 基于错误或不准确的假设进行分析，导致结论的不准确性和误导性。 · 只关注单一的因素或变量，忽略了问题的复杂性和与其他因素的相互影响。
分析.blind_spots_en:
You may selectively collect and use data, ignoring or excluding other important information and data sources.
You may base your analysis on incorrect or inaccurate assumptions, leading to inaccurate and misleading conclusions.
You may focus on only a single factor or variable, ignoring the complexity of the problem and its interactions with other factors.

回顾.standard_definition_zh: “回顾”才干主题突出的人喜欢追溯从前。 他们通过思考过去来了解现状。
回顾.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Context theme enjoy thinking about the past. They understand the present by researching its history.
回顾.feature_zh: 具有回顾主题的人喜欢追溯过去，通过对过去的揣摩来理解现在。
回顾.feature_en: People with strong Context talents enjoy looking back. They understand the present by examining the past.
回顾.description_zh: 你回顾过去是为了了解现在，是为了预测未来。你购买历史书和人物传记，向熟人打听他们的过去经历， 都是为了探索一切的起源。从你的制高点看，当前的变动无常、杂乱无章。只有回顾过去，回到策划之初，当 前才能恢复平稳。过去是一个简明的时代，一个制定蓝图的时代。你回顾过去，目睹蓝图的浮现，再次回到最 初的目标。尽管这些蓝图和初衷后来被装饰得面目全非，难以辨认，但是你回顾过去的主题使它们原貌再现。 这种认识使你充满信心。你消除了干扰，基于对事物本质的了解做出明智决策。通过了解同事们的背景，你能 更好地与他们合作。而且，由于你看到未来的种子已经在过去播下，你对未来更加敏锐。面对新人和新形势， 你需要一些时间来适应。但你必须给自己留出这段时间。你必须推动自己去探索这些问题。你必须让蓝图再次 展现，因为无论情况如何，如果你没有看见蓝图，就无法充分发挥自己的能力。
回顾.description_en: You look to the past to understand the present and to predict the future. You buy history books and biographies, and ask acquaintances about their past experiences, all to explore where everything began. From your vantage point, the present is volatile and chaotic. Only by looking back to the planning stage can the present regain its stability. The past was a simpler time, a time of blueprints. You look back and watch the blueprint emerge, returning to the original goal. Although these blueprints and original intentions were later decorated beyond recognition, your Context theme restores them to their original form. This understanding gives you confidence. You cut through distractions and make wise decisions based on your understanding of the essence of things. By understanding your colleagues' backgrounds, you can work with them more effectively. And because you see that the seeds of the future have already been sown in the past, you are more sensitive to what lies ahead. Faced with new people and new situations, you need some time to adapt. But you must give yourself this time. You must push yourself to explore these questions. You must let the blueprint reappear, because whatever the situation, you cannot fully use your talents unless you can see the blueprint.
回顾.application_zh: 定期回顾自己的工作和生活表现，反思过去的经验和成果，了解自己的优点和改进的领域。 识别自己在工作和生活中取得的成功和优势、技能和特长，认识到自己的价值和潜力。 利用回顾的机会来识别自己的学习需求和发展机会，不断提升自己的技能和知识。 根据自己的反思和目标，制定相应的行动计划，并持续跟踪和评估自己的进展。
回顾.application_en:
Regularly review your work and life performance. Reflect on past experiences and results to understand your strengths and areas for improvement.
Identify your successes and strengths — your skills and talents — in work and life, recognising your value and potential.
Use opportunities to look back to identify your learning needs and development opportunities, continuously improving your skills and knowledge.
Based on your reflection and goals, develop corresponding action plans, and continuously track and evaluate your progress.
回顾.blind_spots_zh: · 苛求自己，对自己的行为和决策持过度批判的态度。这可能导致自我评价过低和自责心理的产生。 · 关注过去的错误和失败，而忽视了自身的成长和进步。他们可能无法看到自己取得的进步和积极变化。 · 回顾自己的过程中缺乏客观观察，过分依赖主观的情绪和偏见。这可能影响对自身行为的准确评估和深入理 解。
回顾.blind_spots_en:
You may be overly critical of yourself and your behaviours and decisions. This can lead to low self-evaluation and feelings of guilt.
You may focus on past mistakes and failures while overlooking your growth and progress. You may be unable to see the progress and positive changes you have made.
In the process of reflection, you may lack objective observation and rely too much on subjective emotions and biases. This can affect the accurate assessment and deeper understanding of your own behaviour.

战略.standard_definition_zh: “战略”才干主题突出的人足智多谋。任何情形下， 他们都能迅速找出相关的规律和要点。
战略.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Strategic theme create alternative ways to proceed. Faced with any given scenario, they can quickly spot the relevant patterns and issues.
战略.feature_zh: 战略主题突出的人具备卓越的智慧和谋略，能快速识别各种方案的模式和结果。
战略.feature_en: People with strong Strategic talents are resourceful. They can quickly identify patterns and outcomes among various options.
战略.description_zh: 战略主题让你能够在日常琐碎中找到前进的快捷方式。它不是一种可教授的技能，而是一种独特的思维方 式和世界观。凭借这种视角，你能够辨识出复杂事物中的规律，将其牢记于心，并尝试各种方案，不断询问自 己：“如果发生这种情况会怎样？如果发生那种情况会怎样？ ”这些问题帮助你预测潜在的障碍并采取精准的判 断。你能清晰地看到各条路径的走向，然后进行筛选。你排除死胡同，摒弃直通障碍的道路，避免通向混乱和 迷茫的路径。你持续不断地进行筛选，直到选定一条路线，这就是你的战略。拥有战略的指引，你开始行动。 这就是你运用战略主题的模式： “如果……会怎样？ ”筛选，行动。
战略.description_en: The Strategic theme allows you to find shortcuts forward amid everyday complexity. It is not a teachable skill but a distinctive way of thinking and seeing the world. With this perspective, you can identify patterns in complex matters, keep them in mind, and try out various options, constantly asking yourself, "What if this happens? What if that happens?" These questions help you predict potential obstacles and make precise judgments. You can clearly see where each path leads, and then you filter. You eliminate dead ends, discard roads that lead straight to obstacles, and avoid paths toward confusion and uncertainty. You keep filtering until you choose a route — that is your strategy. Guided by your strategy, you act. This is your pattern of using the Strategic theme: "What if?" Filter. Act.
战略.application_zh: 制定长期和短期的目标，并确保它们与您的价值观和职业发展路径相符，以实现自己的愿景。 运用战略思维和分析能力，评估当前的工作和生活状况，找到达成目标的最佳途径。 基于您的目标和分析结果，制定战略计划，并为每个步骤制定时间表和资源分配。 执行战略需要坚定的决心和毅力，保持积极的心态和专注力，持之以恒地执行战略计划。
战略.application_en:
Set long-term and short-term goals that align with your values and career path, so you can realise your vision.
Use strategic thinking and analytical skills to assess your current work and life situation and find the best path to your goals.
Based on your goals and analysis, develop strategic plans, including timelines and resource allocation for each step.
Executing strategy requires determination and perseverance. Maintain a positive mindset and focus, persistently carrying out your strategic plans.
战略.blind_spots_zh: · 未能及时感知和适应外部环境的变化，导致战略的失效和跟不上市场的发展。 · 过度自信导致高估自身实力和低估竞争对手，容易陷入盲目行动和错误判断。 · 忽视竞争对手的动态和策略，无法有效应对竞争挑战和制定有效对策。
战略.blind_spots_en:
You may fail to perceive and adapt to changes in the external environment in time, causing your strategy to become ineffective and fall behind market developments.
Overconfidence may lead you to overestimate your own strengths and underestimate competitors, easily falling into blind action and misjudgment.
You may neglect competitors' movements and strategies, leaving you unable to respond effectively to competitive challenges or develop effective countermeasures.

前瞻.standard_definition_zh: 对于有较强前瞻力的人而言，未来令他们心潮澎湃。 他们对未来的憧憬激励着周围的人。
前瞻.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Futuristic theme are inspired by the future and what could be. They energize others with their visions of the future.
前瞻.feature_zh: 对于具有强烈前瞻力的人而言，未来是充满魅力和激情的。他们通过对未来的憧憬来激励和启发身边的 人。
前瞻.feature_en: For people with strong Futuristic talents, the future is captivating and energising. They inspire and motivate those around them through their vision of the future.
前瞻.description_zh: 你喜欢遥望未来，思考着 “如果这样，那该多好？ ”未来让你着迷，它在你眼中犹如墙上的投影，栩栩如 生。这幅细致入微的画面激励着你迈向明天。具体的未来景象因你的优势和兴趣而异 ——更好的产品、更好的 团队、更好的生活或更好的世界 ——但它永远赋予你灵感。你是一位幻想家，能够看到未来的各种可能性，并 珍视这种想象。当现实让你感到困顿，周围的人过于墨守成规时，你唤起对未来的憧憬，激发自己的能量，同 时鼓舞他人。实际上，人们期待你描述关于未来的幻想，他们希望看到一幅画卷，开阔他们的视野，点燃他们 的热情。你能为他们描绘这幅画卷，不断地实践，斟字酌句，让描述更加生动。人们将拥抱你带来的希望。
前瞻.description_en: You love to gaze into the future, thinking, "Wouldn't it be great if...?" The future fascinates you. In your eyes it is like a projection on a wall, vivid and lifelike. This detailed picture motivates you to move toward tomorrow. The specific vision of the future varies with your strengths and interests — better products, better teams, better lives, or a better world — but it always gives you inspiration. You are a visionary, able to see the possibilities of the future and cherishing this imagination. When reality feels confining and those around you are too bound by convention, you call up your vision of the future, re-energising yourself and inspiring others. In fact, people look forward to you describing your vision of the future. They want to see a picture that broadens their horizons and ignites their passion. You can paint that picture for them, practising again and again, choosing your words carefully to make the description more vivid. People will embrace the hope you bring.
前瞻.application_zh: 制定具体、可衡量和有挑战性的目标，为自己和团队设定远大的目标。确保目标与您的价值观和长远愿 景相一致，并将其分解成可行的里程碑和行动步骤。 不仅要关注当前的任务和挑战，还要考虑长远的影响和趋势。预测未来的变化和机会，并制定相应的计 划和策略。 关注行业趋势和新兴技术，探索新的业务模式和市场机会。勇于尝试新的想法和方法，以推动个人和组 织的发展。 将挑战视为学习和成长的机会，灵活适应变化的环境。寻找解决问题的创新方法，勇于采取行动并快速 调整策略。
前瞻.application_en:
Set specific, measurable, and challenging goals for yourself and your team. Make sure your goals align with your values and long-term vision, and break them down into feasible milestones and action steps.
Look beyond current tasks and challenges to consider long-term impact and trends. Anticipate future changes and opportunities, and develop corresponding plans and strategies.
Pay attention to industry trends and emerging technologies, exploring new business models and market opportunities. Be willing to try new ideas and methods to drive personal and organisational development.
View challenges as opportunities for learning and growth. Adapt flexibly to a changing environment. Look for innovative ways to solve problems, be willing to take action, and adjust your strategy quickly.
前瞻.blind_spots_zh: · 过度强调未来的机遇和积极面，可能忽视潜在的风险和困难，导致决策和规划不够全面和谨慎。 · 专注于长远目标和远景，可能忽视了短期内的重要里程碑和目标，影响绩效和工作满意度。 · 过于关注决策的成本和风险，而忽视机遇的成本。他们可能过分考虑可能的损失，而忽视了不行动所带来的 机遇损失。
前瞻.blind_spots_en:
Overemphasising future opportunities and positives may cause you to overlook potential risks and difficulties, leading to decisions and plans that are not comprehensive or cautious enough.
Focusing on long-term goals and vision may cause you to neglect important short-term milestones and goals, affecting performance and job satisfaction.
You may focus too much on the costs and risks of decisions while neglecting the cost of missed opportunities. You may overconsider possible losses while ignoring the opportunity cost of inaction.

搜集.standard_definition_zh: “搜集”才干主题突出的人喜欢搜集和整理。 他们可能会积累信息、观点、标志品，甚至是关系。
搜集.standard_definition_en: People exceptionally talented in the Input theme have a craving to know more. Often they like to collect and archive all kinds of information.
搜集.feature_zh: 搜集主题的人充满好奇心，他们热衷于搜集、整理各种信息，以增长知识和深化理解。
搜集.feature_en: People with strong Input talents are naturally curious. They love gathering, organising, and storing all kinds of information to grow knowledge and deepen understanding.
搜集.description_zh: 你充满好奇心，喜欢收集各种东西。无论是信息还是实物，你都对它们感兴趣。世界多姿多彩、变化无 常，这让你激动不已。你收集信息并非仅仅为了完善理论，而是为了积累更多知识。旅行也是你的喜好，因为 你能在新地方发现独特的文物和趣闻，这些都值得收藏。虽然你无法确定何时或为何需要它们，但你认为它们 可能有各种用途，因此你舍不得丢弃任何东西。因此，你持续不断地收集、整理和储存各种物品，这让你感到 有趣，并让你的思维保持新鲜。也许，某一天，也许就在不久的将来，其中的一些东西会变得珍贵起来。
搜集.description_en: You are naturally curious and like to collect all kinds of things. Whether information or physical objects, you are interested in them. The world is colourful and ever-changing, and that excites you. You collect information not just to refine theories but to accumulate more knowledge. You also enjoy travelling, because you can discover unique artefacts and interesting stories in new places, all worth keeping. Although you cannot be sure when or why you will need them, you believe they may have various uses, so you are reluctant to throw anything away. So you keep collecting, organising, and storing all kinds of things. This feels fun to you and keeps your mind fresh. Perhaps someday, perhaps very soon, some of these things will become valuable.
搜集.application_zh: 参与培训课程、研讨会和工作坊，阅读相关的书籍、文章和研究报告。保持对行业趋势和最新发展的关 注，并持续扩大自己的知识领域。 使用合适的工具，如笔记本、电子表格或知识管理软件，记录和整理重要的观点、事实和数据。建立自 己的信息库，方便日后查阅和回顾。 验证信息的可靠性和准确性，辨别真正有价值的内容。将信息与自己的目标和需求进行关联，筛选出对 工作和生活最有意义的部分。 与他人分享所掌握的知识，可以通过组织内部的培训、团队会议或社交媒体平台等方式进行分享和交 流。 保持对新知识和信息的持续学习。行业和技术的发展日新月异，持续学习是保持竞争力的关键。 将搜集优势主题应用于个人成长和发展。了解自己的兴趣和职业发展目标，有针对性地搜集与之相关的 信息和资源。
搜集.application_en:
Take part in training courses, seminars, and workshops. Read relevant books, articles, and research reports. Stay informed about industry trends and latest developments, and continuously expand your areas of knowledge.
Use appropriate tools such as notebooks, spreadsheets, or knowledge management software to record and organise important viewpoints, facts, and data. Build your own information library for easy future reference and review.
Verify the reliability and accuracy of information, distinguishing what is truly valuable. Connect information with your own goals and needs, filtering out the parts most meaningful to your work and life.
Share the knowledge you have with others through internal training, team meetings, or social media platforms.
Keep learning about new knowledge and information. Industries and technologies change rapidly, and continuous learning is key to staying competitive.
Apply your Input talents to personal growth and development. Understand your interests and career goals, and purposefully collect relevant information and resources.
搜集.blind_spots_zh: · 收集信息时存在偏见或主观判断，导致选择性地获取或过滤信息，影响对问题的客观认知。 · 面对大量信息时无法有效筛选和处理，导致信息过载，影响决策的效率和准确性。 · 由于时间压力，急于搜集信息并做出决策，导致未能充分考虑和评估信息的质量和可行性。
搜集.blind_spots_en:
When collecting information, you may have biases or subjective judgments, selectively acquiring or filtering information and affecting your objective understanding of problems.
When faced with large amounts of information, you may be unable to filter and process it effectively, leading to information overload and affecting the efficiency and accuracy of your decisions.
Because of time pressure, you may rush to gather information and make decisions, failing to fully consider and evaluate the quality and feasibility of the information.
